content.text: integer in Claude API requests that sets the
Claude can generate for a response. It's a
, not a target—Claude will stop early if it completes naturally, but will cut off mid-sentence if it exceeds
. This is different from the model's context window (input tokens):
, while the model's token limit (e.g., 200K for Claude 3/4) controls
high enough for your expected response (1024 for short answers, 4096 for medium, 8192+ for long documents/code), and remember:
Core Concept: Hard Ceiling (Not a Target)

┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ max_tokens: OUTPUT LIMIT ONLY                     │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                     │
│ Input tokens (your prompt):    500 tokens          │
│ Model context window:         200K tokens          │
│ max_tokens:                    1024 tokens         │ ← OUTPUT ONLY
│                                                     │
│ Actual response:               ? tokens (0-1024)   │
│                                                     │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

max_tokens = maximum OUTPUT tokens Claude can generate
If Claude completes naturally, it stops before
Doesn't count input tokens or conversation history
Must be provided in every request

import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

# max_tokens = 100 (very short)
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=100,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Explain quantum computing in detail"}]
)

print(f"Actual tokens used: {message.usage.output_tokens}")
# Output: "Actual tokens used: 100"  ← CUT OFF at limit

print(message.content[0].text)
# "Quantum computing is a type of computation that harnesses quantum mechanical phenomena..."
# [TRUNCATED mid-sentence because it hit 100 tokens]
# max_tokens = 4096 (reasonable)
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=4096,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Explain quantum computing in detail"}]
)

print(f"Actual tokens used: {message.usage.output_tokens}")
# Output: "Actual tokens used: 850"  ← STOPPED EARLY (completed naturally)

print(message.content[0].text)
# [Complete explanation, no truncation]
generate up to 4096. If it completes at 850, it stops there.

# ❌ WRONG - missing max_tokens
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]
)
Some third-party tools or wrappers might have defaults, but the
official SDK requires you to specify it

# ✅ CORRECT - always specify max_tokens
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,  # Always specify
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]
)
Unlike some APIs that use a default like 4096, Anthropic's Messages API
—you must explicitly set it every time.
Full books, extensive codebases, detailed analysis

# max_tokens = 256 (very short)
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=256,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What's the capital of France?"}]
)

print(message.content[0].text)
# "The capital of France is Paris."
Question only needs 1 sentence; 256 is plenty.

# max_tokens = 1024 (standard)
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Explain how neural networks work"}]
)

print(message.content[0].text)
# [Complete explanation, ~500-800 tokens]
Typical default for most chat interactions.
500 words ≈ 600-700 tokens; 4096 gives plenty of room.
Complex code needs lots of tokens for imports, classes, methods, comments.
Claude can generate up to ~750-800 words (1 token ≈ 0.75 words).

- Most chat interactions
- Short explanations
- Q&A responses
- Code snippets

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is machine learning?"}]
)
Claude can generate up to ~3,000 words.

- Blog posts
- Medium-length articles
- Code functions
- Summaries

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=4096,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write a blog post about climate change"}]
)
Claude can generate up to ~6,000 words.

- Long articles
- Full code files
- Detailed reports
- Technical documentation

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=8192,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write a complete Flask API with authentication"}]
)
Full code file with all components, no truncation.
Claude can generate up to ~24,000 words.

- Whitepapers
- Book chapters
- Full codebases
- Extremely detailed analysis

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=32000,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write a complete technical manual"}]
)
Error: "Response exceeded max output token maximum"

# ❌ Will get truncated
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=100,  # Too low
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Explain quantum physics in detail"}]
)

# ✅ Correct
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=4096,  # Higher
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Explain quantum physics in detail"}]
)
Error: "max_tokens: 8192 > 4096, which is the maximum allowed"

# ❌ Model-specific limit exceeded
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",  # Old model
    max_tokens=8192,  # Too high for this model
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]
)
Fix: Use newer model or lower

# ✅ Option 1: Newer model
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",  # Newer model
    max_tokens=8192,  # OK now
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]
)

# ✅ Option 2: Lower max_tokens
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",  # Old model
    max_tokens=4096,  # Within limit
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]
)
To increase limit for older models:

# Use beta header to enable 8192 for Claude 3.5 Sonnet
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
    max_tokens=8192,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}],
    extra_headers={"anthropic-beta": "max-tokens-3-5-sonnet-2024-07-15"}
)

┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ TOKEN LIMITS: TWO DIFFERENT THINGS                 │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                     │
│ Context Window (Input + History):                  │
│   - Claude 3/4: 200K tokens                        │
│   - Controls: input + conversation history         │
│                                                     │
│ max_tokens (Output Only):                          │
│   - You set it (1024, 4096, 8192, etc.)           │
│   - Controls: Claude's response length             │
│                                                     │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Example request:
- Input tokens: 500
- Context window: 200K (holds input + history)
- max_tokens: 1024 (Claude's response limit)
- Output tokens: 850 (actual response)

import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

# Build long conversation history
messages = []
for i in range(100):
    messages.append({"role": "user", "content": f"Question {i}: What is Python?"})
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
        max_tokens=1024,
        messages=messages
    )
    messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": response.content})

# Final request
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,  # Output limit
    messages=messages  # Input + history (up to 200K tokens)
)

→ Claude's final response is limited to 1024 tokens
- Context window (200K) → Holds all 100 previous turns + current input
not factor into OTPM (outputs per minute) rate limit calculations

# Setting max_tokens higher doesn't hurt rate limits
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,  # Rate limit impact: SAME
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]
)

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=32000,  # Rate limit impact: SAME
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]
)

without worrying about rate limits
- Rate limits are based on

value
- No penalty for setting higher limits
high enough for your expected response, and don't worry about "over-provisioning" it.
1. Set High Enough for Your Use Case

# ❌ BAD - too low (will truncate)
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=100,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Explain quantum computing"}]
)

# ✅ GOOD - reasonable for explanation
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=4096,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Explain quantum computing"}]
)
If you're not sure, start with 4096 and adjust down if Claude consistently stops early.
2. Don't Waste Tokens on Tiny Responses

# ❌ BAD - wasteful
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=32000,  # Too high for simple question
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What's 2+2?"}]
)

# ✅ GOOD - efficient
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=256,  # Plenty for "4"
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What's 2+2?"}]
)

# Check model's max output before setting
# Claude 4 Sonnet: 8192 max
# Claude 3 Haiku: 4096 max

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=8192,  # Within model's limit
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write full code"}]
)
Know your model's max output token limit.
For tasks that might exceed even 32K tokens:

# ❌ BAD - might truncate
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=32000,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write a complete 50-page book"}]
)

# ✅ GOOD - segment into parts
parts = []
for i in range(5):
    part = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
        max_tokens=8192,
        messages=[{"role": "user", f"content": f"Write chapter {i+1} of the book"}]
    )
    parts.append(part.content[0].text)

full_book = "\n\n".join(parts)
Break extremely long tasks into chunks.

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=4096,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Explain AI"}]
)

print(f"Requested max: 4096")
print(f"Actual output: {message.usage.output_tokens}")
# Output: "Actual output: 650"  ← STOPPED EARLY
Monitor actual usage to optimize for future requests.

# ❌ BAD - will truncate
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=100,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write a detailed explanation of AI"}]
)

# ✅ GOOD - reasonable
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=4096,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write a detailed explanation of AI"}]
)

# ❌ BAD - wasteful
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=32000,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What's the capital of France?"}]
)

# ✅ GOOD - efficient
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=256,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What's the capital of France?"}]
)

# ❌ BAD - missing max_tokens
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]
)

# ✅ GOOD - include max_tokens
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]
)

# ❌ BAD - exceeds model's max
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",  # 4096 max
    max_tokens=8192,  # Too high
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]
)

# ✅ GOOD - within model's limit
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
    max_tokens=4096,  # Within limit
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]
)
Must be provided in every request (no default)
If Claude completes naturally, it stops before limit
Doesn't count input tokens or conversation history
Setting higher doesn't hurt OTPM limits
Start with 4096 for most use cases, monitor actual usage, and adjust up/down based on whether Claude stops early or truncates.
